zh.lifeSummary: 當Ralph le Boteler於大約1248年出生在英國史洛普爾時，他的父親Ralph le Boteler of Oversley29歲，母親Matilda Pantulf23歲。他和Maud Marmion至少有1個兒子。他去世於1283年12月11日在英國史洛普爾維姆享年36歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾斯塔福德。

zh-hans.Norbury, Stafford Borough, Staffordshire: 英国英格兰斯塔福爾斯塔福德
zh-hans.Oversley Green, Warwickshire, England: 英国瓦維其可爾奧維斯利 格林
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ralph le Boteler于大约1248年出生在英国史洛普爾时，他的父亲Ralph le Boteler of Oversley29岁，母亲Matilda Pantulf23岁。他和Maud Marmion至少有1个儿子。他去世于1283年12月11日在英国史洛普爾維姆享年36岁，埋葬在英国英格兰斯塔福爾斯塔福德。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Boteler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of leather bottles, from an agent derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bouteille ‘bottle’.

nameMeanings.name: Ralph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

variants.name: Ralf
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Ralph le Boteler was born about 1248, in Shropshire, England, his father, Ralph le Boteler of Oversley, was 29 and his mother, Matilda Pantulf, was 23. He had at least 1 son with Maud Marmion. He died on 11 December 1283, in Wem, Shropshire, England, at the age of 36, and was buried in Stafford, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ralph le Boteler est né environ 1248, à Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Ralph le Boteler of Oversley, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Matilda Pantulf, avait 23 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Maud Marmion. Il est décédé le 11 décembre 1283, à Wem, Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 36 ans, et a été enterré à Stafford, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ralph le Boteler um 1248 in Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ralph le Boteler of Oversley 29 und seine Mutter Matilda Pantulf 23. Er hatte mit Maud Marmion mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 11. Dezember 1283 im Alter von 36 Jahren in Wem, Shropshire, England und wurde in Stafford, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ralph le Boteler è nato il circa 1248 nel Shropshire, England, il suo padre, Ralph le Boteler of Oversley, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Matilda Pantulf, aveva 23 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Maud Marmion. Morì il 11 dicembre 1283, nel Wem, Shropshire, England, all'età di 36 anni, e fu sepolto a Stafford, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Ralph le Botelerが約1248年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアで生まれた時，父Ralph le Boteler of Oversleyは29歳，母Matilda Pantulfは23歳であった。彼はMaud Marmionとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1283年12月11日にイギリスシュロプシュアイアウエムで36歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアスタフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Ralph le Boteler(이)가 대략 1248년 영국 슈럽실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ralph le Boteler of Oversley(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda Pantulf(은)는 23세였다. 그는 Maud Marmion(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1283년 12월 11일 영국 슈럽실르 베에서 36세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 스태퍼드에 묻혔다.

mn.Norbury, Stafford Borough, Staffordshire: Stafford, Stæffordscīr, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ralph le Boteler nasceu aproximadamente 1248, em Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Ralph le Boteler of Oversley, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Matilda Pantulf, tinha 23 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Maud Marmion. Ele faleceu em 11 de dezembro de 1283, em Wem, Shropshire, Inglaterra, com 36 anos, e foi sepultado em Stafford, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ralph le Boteler родился около 1248 г. в местности шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Ralph le Boteler of Oversley было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda Pantulf, 23. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Maud Marmion. Он умер 11 декабря 1283 г. в местности вем, шропшир, Англия в возрасте 36 лет и был похоронен в местности стаффорд, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ralph le Boteler nació aproximadamente 1248, en Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Ralph le Boteler of Oversley, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Matilda Pantulf, tenía 23 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Maud Marmion. Murió el 11 de diciembre de 1283, en Wem, Shropshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 36 años, y fue sepultado en Stafford, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
